kept.text:
$RBRK Check with CIO of Global Manufacturing Company: Deepening Investment in Rubrik in 2026 - Scotiabank 

OUR TAKE: We recently spoke with the Global CIO of a >$20B annual revenue manufacturing enterprise who highlighted their core IT priorities for 2026, in their ~$360M IT budget which is due to be flat YY. Initiatives include replacing a legacy backup and recovery vendor with Rubrik - with spend scaling to a $6-7M run rate in 2026, and could reach $10M in 2027. We view this conversation as clear validation of Rubrik’s security-first architecture as a key competitive differentiator, as well as a nice reminder of the massive displacement opportunity ahead in the data protection, backup and recovery market. We estimate Rubrik currently holds just over 10% of the market, with ~40% still up for grabs from legacy vendors.

Rubrik is replacing Dell, also Rubrik beat Commvault, Veeam, and Cohesity in a direct competitive evaluation.

author.name: Jim Cramer
author.description:
Host of @madmoneyoncnbc and I run the CNBC Investing Club. 

My new book is out now: https://t.co/autOFQ2NP0

kept.text:
AI: NET GOOD OR NET BAD FOR CYBERSECURITY?

After 30 years in IT, I’ve seen that the greatest opportunities often carry the greatest risks. 

AI is no different.

AI creates 100X more opportunity for everyone, but it also creates 100X more risk.

To mitigate that risk, organizations need a framework to govern AI.

That framework should give them confidence in scaling AI - while ensuring that they can deploy agents to help them scale faster and become more competitive.

That's why we launched Rubrik Agent Cloud, which accelerates agentic operations while managing risk. 

We don’t yet know if AI is a net good or a net bad for cybersecurity. 

But we do know how to drive the technology in the right direction.

And that’s by unleashing agents, not risk.
